market_summary: Non-rebalance day: Day 11 of systematic weekly hold period following February 12 portfolio rebalance, next rebalance window opens February 26 per 7-day minimum spacing cadence

Critical data retrieval failure: API errors prevented fetching current prices for all 8 portfolio holdings (XOM, CVX, LLY, JPM, XLI, KLAC, AVGO, GLD) and benchmarks (SPY, QQQ), only CVX-USD crypto ticker returned (irrelevant to equity CVX holding)

Systematic hold discipline enforced: Unable to assess intraday portfolio performance or benchmark comparison due to data outage, but rebalance cadence policy requires no action regardless of price movements until February 26 window

thesis: Systematic weekly hold discipline enforced: Day 11 following February 12 full portfolio rebalance, rebalance cadence requires 7-day minimum spacing with next opportunity opening February 26

Data retrieval failure does not override process discipline: API errors prevented current price verification for all holdings and benchmarks, but 7-day aggregated context shows stable portfolio construction through February 22 with no thesis-damaging developments

Prior session momentum remains constructive: Friday February 21 marked Day 9 with broad market strength (SPY +0.50%, QQQ +0.68%), energy surge (XOM +3.28%, CVX +1.91%), gold rebound (GLD +2.25% to $458.28), quality names stable, validating portfolio positioning entering weekend
risks: Data blackout risk: Complete API failure on February 23 prevents real-time monitoring of portfolio holdings, benchmark performance, or intraday thesis validation—relying on stale February 21-22 context

Weekend gap risk unobservable: Unable to assess Sunday-Monday price action or news flow that may have created material moves in energy (27% allocation), technology (20% allocation), or gold hedge (18% allocation)

Systematic discipline creating blind execution: Enforcing no-action policy without current data visibility could miss acute thesis damage, though 7-day aggregated history shows no recurring red flags and next rebalance window opens in 3 days
falsifiable_checks: If AVGO closes below $305 before February 26 rebalance window, emergency review warranted (last verified $332.85 on February 22, +$27.85 cushion)
If GLD falls below $440 before February 26 rebalance, reassess 18% safe-haven allocation sizing (last verified $462.62 on February 22, +$22.62 cushion)
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Day 11 of systematic weekly hold period with complete API data retrieval failure preventing current price verification. Process discipline requires 7-day minimum spacing to avoid reactive management and transaction cost drag. Prior 7-day aggregated context (February 16-22) shows stable portfolio construction: gold hedge delivering exceptional performance (GLD +7.9% cumulative peak, +10.2% by February 22), energy allocation weathering normal sector rotation, quality names resilient. Last verified session (February 21) showed constructive momentum with SPY +0.50%, QQQ +0.68%, energy surge (XOM +3.28%, CVX +1.91%), gold rebound (+2.25%). All falsifiable thresholds maintained healthy cushions as of February 22. Premature action based on incomplete data would sacrifice systematic edge for reactive timing risk. Next rebalance window February 26 allows full data refresh and proper IC evaluation.

constraints_check.notes: Portfolio unchanged from February 22: Energy 27% (XOM 15%, CVX 12%), Technology 20% (KLAC 10%, AVGO 10%), Commodities 18% (GLD), Healthcare 12% (LLY), Financials 12% (JPM), Industrials 11% (XLI). Max position 18% (GLD), max sector 27% (Energy), crypto 0%. All constraints satisfied. Factor score highlights UNKNOWN due to API failure preventing momentum/fundamental refresh. Risk budget: Energy concentration 27% remains primary portfolio risk, gold hedge 18% at elevated levels post-parabolic move, technology 20% split across semis/infrastructure. Scenario stress UNKNOWN without current price data. Execution plan: Systematic hold through February 26 rebalance window regardless of intraday moves. Monitoring triggers: AVGO <$305 emergency threshold, GLD <$440 hedge reassessment, but current verification impossible due to data outage. Kill-switch: Override hold discipline only for verifiable thesis-damaging events (earnings miss, guidance cut, macro shock), none evidenced in 7-day context through February 22.
